_comment: TRADE-ALL + PRICE-CAP config (REAL MONEY, deployed 2026-06-29, user-authorized). Reverted flat_favorite -> fav_convergence (trade every favorite in [buy_p, sell_p], hold to settle, stop_p=0). buy_p 0.76; sell_p LOWERED 0.93->0.85 to skip the overpriced 0.85-0.93 high band (margin ~0/negative). WHY: the flat strategy was ~breakeven on regime-matched recent data (entries drifted up to ~0.87 where margin~0); the +8pp was a 6-day-archive average that didn't hold live. A sell_p sweep on BOTH recent AND the 6-day archive showed 0.76-trade-all is significantly +EV on every config (CI excludes 0), and capping sell_p to 0.85 RAISES per-trade EV (+4.0%->+5.2%) + lowers buy price (0.82->0.79) + on recent data is the best total$ (+27 vs +24); robust across both datasets. TRADE-OFF vs flat: ~74% fire (high) but higher variance/drawdown than flat in bad regimes. CAVEAT: still non-stationary (trade-all was -EV in the oldest archive regime). max_slip_below execution gate still active. flat_favorite + 0.76-fav_hold backups on box.
